reviews.review_description:
Got this free, thanks.
Music
Overall nice, like the idea of speeding the music when speed bonuses on.

Controller
Tried with oculus go, sideways. Itse not optimal for immersion and fun but works

Game
Needs more content, 
curves, hills, possibility to go risky secret way

Needs timer: best own time, Overall score, global ranking table

There could be two players game too. 


Did not like
Returns To level beginning if hit the object. Better solution would be add minus time.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to write a review, and I'm glad your controller got repaired! Hopefully you'll find future content updates and my next game more to your liking. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: I like the view of the endless street. You can also hold your controller like you normally would, this works out as well. If you crash into an object a kind of animation would be nice instead of a text. And yes the pizza deliverer also would need a bit of animation, preferably the board tilted. But - it's fun:-)

I did the tutorial, then started off on challenging difficulty. I’m like ‘hey, this is simple, yet fun. Very nice’. The. I got to level 3 or 4 in campaign and I’m laughing as I’m flying down the track, narrowly dodging obstacles and barely cross the finish line. Can’t wait to play again. Don’t judge until level 3 or 4.
I really enjoyed playing Speed Surfer. Simple but fun game.
This game has a wacky approach to a simple game. It is very easy to learn and understand, but you get addicted to the challenge of just one more delivery.
A novel use for the controller, fun quirky graphics and great sound and humour make this a no-brainer! The game is a fun avoiding obstacle game, and there is a secret behind you too! :-)
Kind of a simpler Subway Surfers. Gets pretty challenging quickly.  Go controller works fine.
Hi dev this is really cool games some levels are hard but that’s part of the game I will keep on playing it to complete the levels it’s really fun.
This game is fast, fun, and a trip. I like games like this in the morning they help me wake up :) Just one more Pizza to deliver.
Very cool concept. Definitely fun. Highly recommended!
simple and fun game with crispy graphics, well done.
This is a simple, but addicting game.  I’d like to see hills and curves added for a 5th star.

If this were a typical mobile game for my phone I probably would pass it up, but games like this constantly remind me that VR has the potential to take good, even simple ideas and make them feel VERY FRESH and enjoyable. I’ve played many endless runner games, but not in VR. My brain doesn’t process this game in the same way as something similar that I would play on my iPad. The graphics are simple and do the job; controlling my surfboard man was surprisingly easy with the Oculus Go controller, and when I hit something and died I knew that it was MY fault, not due to the inaccuracy of the controller. I enjoy playing this for 10 minutes or so at a time before I go to bed.

What a fun and somewhat addictive game.  It really reminds me of that racing game from Coleco, or their football game.  Or for you more modern folks, Temple Run.  It captures that same game play  BUT because it's VR, you really feel like you are there.  Easy to learn to play BUT real tough to master.  The best kind of game.

This game is for those who love to go fast and push themselves to the limit of hoverboard surfing to deliver pizzas. So pretty much everyone. I had lots of fun with this and even have gameplay here: https://youtu.be/yeT4nngIC54

reviews.review_title: Entertaining For Short Bursts Of Fun
reviews.review_description:
Speed Surfer: Superhero uses his speed surfing ability to deliver pizza. An interesting yet silly idea giving this simple game a simple story that makes it more memorable.

Gameplay - Perfect for short sessions when time is an issue or looking to cure some boredom. As the player, you accelerate forward dodging the obstacles while collecting power ups for speed bursts and shields. Goal is to reach the end (100%) of the “road” to safely deliver the pizza.

Control - Using the Oculus Go Controller (I played by gripping the controller normally) hold trigger to accelerate and move the controller left and right to move the Surfer left and right. Controls felt responsive and served their intended purpose.

Graphics - Nice resolution, smooth flowing (high FPS), crisp, and colorful. Was amused with the message located behind you (turn around to see).

Sound - Fit in nicely and complimented the gameplay and graphics.

Overall - Speed Surfer is a fun time killer that is worthy of being added to your Oculus library. I look forward to seeing future updates and new game material that the developer releases. Now get out there and deliver some pizzas!

UPDATED REVIEW: The added first person view is GREATLY welcomed and very enjoyable. Adds a level of newness while also upping the fun-factor of the game and is enjoyable to see and dodge the obstacles up close. Keep up the great work!
A simple but fun game that can get addicting. It's great for short sessions and it runs smoothly.

This game is a bit barebones (by the creator's own admission) but the core gameplay is very fun.  The controls took me a little while to get the hang of, but after adjusting to the unconventional method of holding the controller, it actually turned out to be very responsive and satisfying. One tip: at first it felt like the sensitivity kept dropping out on me, but I realized it was because I was letting the controller tilt forward too far, which seemed to make it lose track of its orientation.

My main critique of the game is that, while it's very fun to speed through the obstacles at high speed, there's no actual incentive to go fast. Because it doesn't matter how quickly you finish a level, you're actually sort of punished for going quickly—the speed power ups just make the level harder, and conversely the patches that slow you down help you avoid danger. If there were a time limit on the levels, it would add more urgency and reward you for going as fast as possible.

Overall enjoyable, and there's a lot of room for improvement if the developer wants to polish the experience further.
I like the quick pace and interesting levels.  Nothing to complain about from me.
Fun theme. I mean, we are delivering 🍕, it doesn't get much cooler. Great controls, great optimization, keep up the good work!
